channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Os três rejeitados: Alcolumbre, Messias e o STF
item.description: After more than four months of deadlock, President Lula sent to the Senate the message formalizing the nomination of Jorge Messias to the vacant seat on the Supreme Federal Court following the early retirement of Luís Roberto Barroso. The nomination comes after previous rejections of Alcolumbre and other names, highlighting political difficulties in the selection process. Messias, the current Attorney General of the Union, is seen as a technical figure aligned with the government.

item.title: É #FAKE que Lula recomendou substituir carro por caminhada após preço de combustível subir; fala foi sobre uso de Ozempic
item.description: The video circulating on social media falsely attributes to Lula a recommendation to replace car use with walking due to rising fuel prices. In reality, the statement was made during an inauguration event at the Federal Hospital of Andaraí in Rio de Janeiro, discussing Ozempic and the importance of physical activity for weight loss, with no reference to fuel prices or related policies.

item.title: Planalto quer estratégia para evitar contaminação eleitoral sobre ida de Messias ao STF
item.description: The Lula government is pushing to fast-track Jorge Messias' Supreme Court nomination for a Senate hearing in the first half of 2026, fearing electoral calendar interference and Senate opposition. Allies aim to secure approval before the elections, while opposition forces seek to delay the vote, betting on a Lula defeat and the potential to overturn the nomination.

item.title: Copa 2026: Quem sobe e quem desce na briga pelas vagas finais da seleção após a última data Fifa antes da convocação
item.description: Brazil's friendlies ahead of the 2026 World Cup call-up served as the final chance for players to secure spots in the 26-man squad. Danilo Santos (Botafogo) and Endrick stood out against Croatia, with Danilo emerging as a leading option in midfield and Endrick contributing a penalty-winning goal and an assist. Others like Igor Thiago and Ibañez fulfilled specific roles, while defense, midfield and attack remain open positions ahead of the May 18 announcement.

item.title: Artigo: polarizar contra a polarização é caminho para gerar credibilidade entre eleitores independentes
item.description: Brazilian independent voters (33%) seek an alternative to the Lula-Bolsonaro polarization but lack candidates who represent their desire. While Bolsonarists and non-Bolsonarist right exhibit homogeneous affect, independents emotionally align closer with non-Lulist left, suggesting that polarizing against polarization could build credibility with this group. Plaza Pública Survey (Dec 2025) data shows independents have lower adherence to the Instability territory (34% vs 38% overall) and equal to the Potential territory (16%), placing them emotionally between the extremes.

item.title: Governistas do MDB demonstram insatisfação por Lula não ter feito proposta formal para compor chapa à reeleição
item.description: MDB officials close to the government expressed dissatisfaction that President Lula did not make a formal invitation for the party to join his re-election ticket as vice-presidential candidate. They interpret the lack of a direct gesture as a sign the administration prefers a pragmatic approach, settling for MDB neutrality rather than a formal alliance. Consequently, the party is likely to remain nationally neutral while allowing state-level factions to pursue local alliances, such as São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes’ support for Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Em clima de eleição antecipada, Paes explora 'herança maldita' de Castro, e Ruas tenta colar imagem a Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Eduardo Paes (PSD) and Douglas Ruas (PL) are shaping early campaign strategies for a potential supplementary election in Rio de Janeiro, aiming to replace Cláudio Castro (PL) before year-end. Paes emphasizes Castro’s 'cursed inheritance,' criticizing police politicization and fiscal disorder, while Ruas aligns himself with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) to nationalize the race. The prospect of a June direct election has gained traction following STF ministers’ remarks and is now openly backed by PL and PSD.

item.title: CPI do Crime Organizado vira novo front do caso Master após fim de comissão que investigava INSS
item.description: The Organized Crime CPI approved measures expanding the investigation into Banco Master, including summoning former Federal District governor Ibaneis Rocha, breaking banking and fiscal secrecy for Fabiano Campos Zettel and Prime You, and calling former Rio governor Cláudio Rio and judge Macário Ramos Júdice Neto. The actions target money laundering, organized crime financing, and alleged information leaks to criminal factions, focusing on the network around banker Daniel Vorcaro.

item.title: Despedida de ministros tem tom eleitoral, críticas à Sidônio e Alckmin confirmado como vice; saiba as mudanças na Esplanada
item.description: President Lula announced that Geraldo Alckmin will remain his running mate for re-election, ending speculation of an alliance with the MDB. During a ministerial meeting, 18 ministers planning to run in the 2026 elections were honored, while Chief of Staff Rui Costa sharply criticized the government’s communications management under Sidônio Palmeira.

item.title: Entrevista: líder do PT diz que Hugo Motta terá que fazer campanha para Lula ‘se quiser se reeleger’
item.description: PT congressional leader Pedro Uczai states that Chamber President Hugo Motta (Republicanos-PB) must support Lula to secure re-election in 2026, given Paraíba's strong Lula base. While Motta shows alignment with the government, Uczai stresses he must preserve institutional independence until the official campaign begins, and argues that right-wing reorganization does not disrupt Lula's electoral advantage.

item.title: Caiado escala deputado evangélico como articulador e inicia contato com líderes religiosos para crescer no segmento
item.description: Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) has launched his presidential pre-candidacy and is initiating an evangelical voter outreach strategy led by deputy Otoni de Paula (PSD-RJ). The plan includes participation in pastor conventions, private meetings with religious leaders, influential dinners, and building communication channels within evangelical churches, starting with Bishop Samuel Ferreira of the Assembleia de Deus, with plans to expand to denominations like the Universal, World Power of God, and Grace of God churches, as well as influencers such as Silas Malafaia, aiming to position Caiado as a less hardline Bolsonarist alternative within the right-wing spectrum.

item.title: STF suspende afastamento do prefeito de Sorocaba
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Nunes Marques suspended the removal of Sorocaba's mayor Rodrigo Manga, ordered during Operation Copia e Cola over alleged health contract fraud. The ruling cited 'excessive intervention' in municipal politics and warned that delay could further hinder Manga's potential gubernatorial bid in São Paulo, with the disqualification deadline on April 4.

item.title: Eleições 2026: o que é cláusula de desempenho dos partidos e como isso impacta a disputa eleitoral; entenda
item.description: The performance clause for political parties, also known as the electoral threshold, sets minimum vote or representation requirements for parties to access public funding and broadcast time. Since its implementation, it has reduced the number of parties represented in the Chamber of Deputies from 30 in 2018 to 17 today, encouraging mergers and party federations. For the 2026 elections, the thresholds have been raised: parties must elect 13 federal deputies across at least one-third of the federal units or obtain 2.5% of valid votes, with a minimum of 1.5% in nine states.

item.title: Após denúncia da PGR, Silvio Almeida fala em 'acusações irresponsáveis'
item.description: Former Human Rights Minister Silvio Almeida posted a video on social media last night, claiming he is an 'innocent man' regarding the accusations brought by the PGR and criticizing how President Lula dismissed him. He described the allegations as 'irresponsible'.

item.title: Medidas para turbinar Lula têm potencial limitado
item.description: Lula's assessment in the 'farewell' ministerial meeting revealed concerns about declining popularity after over a year of shifts in government communication strategy, indicating an 'empty tank' in public perception. The subscriber-only article highlights the limited potential of measures aimed at boosting his image amid eroding popular support.

item.title: Lula: Alckmin de vice e discurso de candidato - O Assunto #1692
item.description: President Lula signaled his 2026 re-election bid by confirming Geraldo Alckmin as his running mate and announcing a reshuffle of 14 ministries, including the Chief of Staff, Education, Planning, and Environment. He also submitted Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF and emphasized contrasting his government with Bolsonaro's as the core campaign strategy.

item.title: Lula envia ao Senado indicação de Jorge Messias para a vaga de Barroso no STF; veja próximos passos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has formally submitted the nomination of Jorge Messias, Attorney General of the Union, to the Senate for a vacancy on the Supreme Federal Court left by the retirement of Minister Luís Roberto Barroso in October 2024. Although the choice was announced in November 2025, the formal message was only sent now, after 130 days, triggering the constitutional process of review by the Senate’s Constitution and Justice Committee, followed by a secret vote requiring 41 senators’ approval for confirmation.

item.title: Governo obtém apoio da maioria dos estados e avança em medidas para evitar crise nos combustíveis
item.description: The federal government and a majority of Brazilian states have advanced joint measures to prevent a surge in fuel prices in 2026, amid the prolonged Middle East war and its impact on global oil prices. The actions aim to curb price volatility during an election year, with broad support from state governments.

item.title: Carlos e Jair Renan devem fazer primeira visita a Bolsonaro após ida para prisão domiciliar; Flávio não deve ir
item.description: Carlos Bolsonaro and Jair Renan Bolsonaro are expected to visit their father, Jair Bolsonaro, this Wednesday, marking the first family visit since his transfer to house arrest. The visit occurs during the first permitted window under judicial rules allowing family access only on Wednesdays and Saturdays in specific time slots. Flávio Bolsonaro is not expected to attend, as he, acting as his father’s lawyer, can meet him daily for up to 30 minutes in Brasília, reducing the need for regular family visits.

item.title: Chá de revelação do PSD sepulta ilusão de 'terceira via' kassabista
item.description: Gilberto Kassab announced Ronaldo Caiado as the PSD's presidential candidate, ending hopes for a 'third way' Kassabist alternative. Caiado, a patriarch of rural right-wing politics, previously ran in the 1989 presidential election, finishing tenth with 0.7% of the vote. The article, available only to subscribers, underscores the PSD's alignment with conservative agribusiness forces.

item.title: Tucanos convidam Leite a voltar ao partido para se candidatar a presidente
item.description: PSDB leaders sent messages and called Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite, inviting him to return to the party to run for president in 2026. Leite is currently affiliated with the PSD but has been asked to rejoin the tucanos to lead a presidential bid.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro segue os passos do pai e planta sementes da desconfiança eleitoral
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), a pre-candidate for president in 2026, spoke at the CPAC in the U.S., following his father’s strategy of sowing distrust in Brazil’s electoral system. His speech reinforced baseless claims of election fraud to mobilize the conservative base, despite offering no evidence. This comes as some still hope for a moderate Bolsonarism — a prospect increasingly undermined by such actions.

item.title: Caiado entrou na disputa
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado entered the 2026 presidential race by proposing amnesty for those convicted in the 2022/2023 coup plot, aiming to attract Flávio Bolsonaro’s voter base as a foundation for a third-way alternative. He criticized political polarization, stating that only those who benefit from it fuel it. The viability of this path will unfold in the coming months.

item.title: Grupo de ministros se irrita com Fachin e fala em presidente do STF desmoralizado
item.description: Statements by the STF president regarding judges' errors, code of conduct, and the end of the fake news inquiry irritated ministers of the court, who complain about public embarrassment and inappropriate exposure of internal tribunal dilemmas. The remarks were made to the press on Tuesday, March 31, 2026.

item.title: Defesa falha, mas Brasil reage no fim e garante vitória sobre a Croácia antes da convocação final para Copa
item.description: Brazil defeated Croatia 3-1 in Orlando in a friendly match ahead of the 2026 World Cup final squad selection, with goals from Danilo, Igor Thiago (penalty), and Martinelli. Despite a defensive error by goalkeeper Bento that led to Croatia's goal, Brazil responded strongly in the closing moments, highlighted by standout performances from Danilo, Vini Jr, Luiz Henrique, and Endrick. Coach Carlo Ancelotti made six changes from the loss to France as part of preparations for the final roster.

item.title: Manifesto pede candidatura de Eduardo Leite à Presidência por outro partido
item.description: A group of economists, former parliamentarians, and civil leaders launched a manifesto urging Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) to run for president in 2026 under another party, after the PSD nominated Ronaldo Caiado as its pre-candidate. The manifesto, titled 'For a Possible Brazil and a President Equal to the Challenge,' calls for economically managed governance free from interest group capture, technical leadership in health and education, and institutional reform of the Three Branches within 90 days of inauguration. Leite said he will fulfill his gubernatorial mandate until December 2026 and will not publicly contest the PSD’s decision.

item.title: Fachin busca “consenso possível” no STF sobre eleição no Rio
item.description: Supreme Federal Court President Edson Fachin stated that the Court will seek a 'possible consensus' in the April 8 ruling on the format of Rio de Janeiro's elections. He emphasized that consensus leads to more viable decisions and that the goal is to determine whether an agreement among the involved parties is achievable.

item.title: Eduardo Leite parabeniza Caiado, mas não declara apoio ao pré-candidato do PSD à Presidência
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) congratulated former Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) on being chosen as the party's presidential pre-candidate, but did not explicitly endorse his candidacy. The message was posted on social media on Tuesday, March 31.

item.title: Alesp aprova segunda parte de pacote da segurança de Tarcísio com reajuste de salário a policiais
item.description: São Paulo's Legislative Assembly approved two bills from Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' security package: a 10% linear salary raise for police (excluding prison agents) and a restructuring of the Military Police career, both passed under urgency rules. The move aims to ease police discontent following a veto on a retirement bill, but draws criticism for omitting prison police and introducing subjective criteria like 'moral idoneity' in promotions, which civil society groups warn could politicize the forces. The monthly fiscal impact is estimated at R$ 130 million.

item.title: Artilharia contra Bolsonaro pode viabilizar rivais
item.description: PL president Valdemar Costa Neto dismissed the PSD's recent announcement of Ronaldo Caiado's presidential candidacy, signaling inter-party tension. A São Paulo poll showed high rejection for several political figures, including Caiado, Lula, Haddad, Nunes, and Bolsonaro family members, suggesting that concentrated criticism of Bolsonaro could create opportunities for his rivals.

item.title: Alckmin, o chaveiro de Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva confirmed that Geraldo Alckmin will remain as his running mate for re-election in 2026. Although Lula explored other potential candidates, none of the parties he approached showed interest in replacing Alckmin, who thus retains his position on the ticket.

item.title: Fachin diz que STF buscará 'consenso possível' em julgamento sobre eleições no Rio
item.description: Supreme Federal Court (STF) President Edson Fachin stated that the Court will seek a 'possible consensus' in the ruling on the format of indirect elections in Rio de Janeiro, following the resignation of Governor Cláudio Castro. Despite divisions among justices — with an initial majority favoring secret voting and a 24-hour disengagement period, but dissents from Alexandre de Moraes, Zanin, Gilmar Mendes, and Flávio Dino advocating for direct elections — Fachin emphasized he will work internally to enable a collegiate decision, even if unanimity is unlikely.

item.title: Rui Costa diz que Flávio Bolsonaro quer entregar riquezas aos EUA
item.description: Chief of Staff Rui Costa (PT) accused Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) of proposing to deliver Brazil’s wealth to the United States, interpreting his remarks at CPAC in Texas about partnership in critical minerals as an attempt to subject Brazil to U.S. influence. Costa, who will leave government to run for Senate in Bahia, said this amounts to endorsing a potential U.S. intervention in Brazil. He also denied any government communication strategy targeting other candidates, despite a technical tie between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in Paraná Pesquisas polls.

item.title: Rui Costa volta atrás em críticas a Sidônio na comunicação do governo
item.description: Minister of the Civil House Rui Costa walked back previous criticisms of the government's communication and praised Sidônio Palmeira, Secretary of Social Communication of the Presidency, saying he had brought a 'positive turn'. Earlier, in a ministerial meeting, Costa questioned whether the public knew the government's achievements, amid polls showing high rejection of Lula and a technical tie with Flávio Bolsonaro in the 2026 election.

item.title: Lula faz afago à PF e promete valorização em meio a momento tenso com corporação
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva pledged to present a salary valuation proposal for federal police officers amid their mobilization. He urged government technicians to act quickly on the measure, aiming to ease tensions between the executive branch and the police force.

item.title: Caiado terá de bater muito em Bolsonaro filho para não ser assistente de palco do show da direita
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado's presidential launch by the PSD garnered little public attention, particularly on social media, amid a scarcity of politically relevant news. The article argues that to stand out on the right, Caiado must directly confront figures like Bolsonaro and his allies, rather than becoming a mere sidekick in the far-right narrative.

item.title: Lula diz que governo fará de tudo para conter preço do diesel
item.description: President Lula said the government will do everything possible to contain diesel prices, criticizing the war in Iran and its impact on international oil prices. He highlighted measures such as Federal Police and Public Prosecutor's Office oversight, and blamed the previous administration for selling BR Distribuidora, which he said prevents Petrobras' price cuts from reaching consumers.

item.title: "Guerra é do Trump, não é do povo brasileiro", diz Lula
item.description: President Lula criticized the war in Iran, blaming Trump and stating that the Brazilian people should not bear the consequences of the conflict, such as rising diesel prices. He said the government is taking possible measures to prevent diesel price increases, including oversight by the Federal Police and Public Prosecutor’s Office, and urged the five permanent UN Security Council members to act responsibly, accusing them of waging war instead of preserving peace.

item.title: “Mais um petista fiel no Supremo”, diz Novo sobre Messias
item.description: The Novo party criticized the nomination of Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court, labeling him a 'faithful petista' due to his close ties with Lula's government. Messias was nominated to fill the vacancy left by Luís Roberto Barroso, whose early departure opened a seat on the Court. The criticisms were shared on social media by Novo, reflecting concerns over the alleged politicization of the Judiciary.

item.title: Lula afirma que elite brasileira “não queria que o povo estudasse”
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva claimed that Brazil's elite historically opposed popular education, citing the 1920 establishment of the first federal university as evidence of national backwardness. He compared Brazil unfavorably to other Latin American and Central American countries, despite inaccurately stating that Santo Domingo University was founded in 1532. Lula argued that expanding access to higher education is crucial for national development, highlighting initiatives such as Prouni, federal institutes, and the Universidade para Todos program.

item.title: Novo ministro da Fazenda quer automatizar declaração anual do IR
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan proposed automating the annual Income Tax declaration, replacing manual filing with a system that aggregates data from official and private sources, requiring only taxpayer validation. The initiative aims to simplify the tax system, reduce bureaucracy, and promote innovation, with an estimated reach of 60% of taxpayers. The change will be gradual, building on the current pre-filled declaration model.

item.title: Queixas sobre comunicação de Lula às vésperas da campanha provocam tensão na despedida dos ministros
item.description: Civil House Minister Rui Costa criticized the Lula government's communication ahead of the 2026 election campaign, noting that over 40% of the population gets information via WhatsApp groups and urging a comparative strategy between Lula and Bolsonaro administrations to highlight current government achievements. He insisted the public has the right to know favorable data, describing the shift as 'from water to wine'. Secom chief Sidônio Palmeira acknowledged past communication failures, particularly in countering fake news, and advocated for proactive messaging to prevent false narratives from taking hold.

item.title: Divã: Em busca do sexto acesso, Guto Ferreira ironiza soluções estrangeiras no Brasil: 'O milagre está acontecendo?'
item.description: Guto Ferreira, head coach of Vila Nova, aims to lead the team to Brasileirão Série A in 2026, having previously achieved promotion with Remo, Sport, Internacional, Ponte Preta and Bahia. He criticized the lack of contractual protection in clubs that do not retain squads after promotion, stating he performs better at lower-budget teams with mid-table results. The interview also includes excerpts from a conversation with Zico about player development and Neymar and Paquetá's World Cup prospects.

item.title: Grupo de economistas e políticos quer Leite na corrida presidencial
item.description: A group of economists, former parliamentarians, and civic leaders is organizing a manifesto urging Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite to run for president in 2026 under a different party, after being excluded from the presidential race by his own party. The effort, reported by Folha de S.Paulo, aims to overcome the party veto and secure Leite’s participation in the election.

item.title: Lula critica privatização da BR Distribuidora e diz que ‘atravessadores’ impedem queda no preço do diesel
item.description: President Lula criticized the 2021 privatization of BR Distribuidora by the Bolsonaro government, stating that 'middlemen' prevent diesel price reductions from Petrobras from reaching consumers. He said the government is using regulatory bodies like ANP, Senacon, and the Federal Police to combat abusive fuel price hikes, citing the 'Vem Diesel' operation which issued 16 infringement notices. Lula also blamed the U.S. and Israel for the global diesel price surge due to their attacks on Iran, asserting that Brazil should not be a victim of foreign conflicts.

item.title: Aposta de Lula em Minas, Pacheco se filia ao PSB nesta quarta
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) will announce his affiliation with the PSB this Wednesday (1st) in Brasília, with expectations of running for governor of Minas Gerais in an alliance with President Lula. Despite pressure from the Planalto and PT’s state-level mobilization, Pacheco has not yet officially confirmed his candidacy, emphasizing the need to consult other parties, including the PSDB, to build a broad center coalition.

item.title: BR-319: governo Lula autoriza avanço da pavimentação de via na Amazônia que gerou bate-boca de senadores com Marina
item.description: The Lula government has authorized the start of paving works on the BR-319 highway in the Amazon, including the construction of a bridge over the Igapó-Açu River and the bidding process for the 339.4 km middle section with an estimated investment of R$ 678 million. The project, enabled by legislative changes in December 2023 that expedite environmental licensing, is backed by the president and part of the PAC, but faces criticism from environmentalists and Indigenous groups over risks of deforestation, land grabbing, and invasions, with reports of over 3,500 km of illegal roads already encroaching on conservation units and Indigenous territories.

item.title: Reajuste para forças de segurança de DF e ex-territórios segue para sanção
item.description: The Senate approved MP 1.326/2025, which adjusts the remuneration of public security forces in the Federal District and the former federal territories of Amapá, Rondônia, and Roraima. Reported by Senator Randolfe Rodrigues (PT-AP), the measure is now awaiting presidential sanction and has already been partially implemented in December 2025 and January 2026. Senators emphasized the raise as a just recognition of security personnel and the culmination of a long-standing advocacy effort.

item.title: Pacheco fecha com PSB e aproxima Lula de palanque em Minas
item.description: Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) will join the PSB on April 1, 2026, leaving the PSD to run for governor of Minas Gerais. The move is part of Lula's strategy to build a competitive electoral base in Brazil's second-largest electorate. Pacheco had awaited a possible concession from MDB and União Brasil before deciding, but ultimately chose the PSB, which meets his requirement of presidential election neutrality and state directory autonomy.

item.title: Senado aprova projeto que define regras para guarda compartilhada de pets em caso de divórcio
item.description: The Brazilian Federal Senate approved a bill establishing rules for shared pet custody in cases of divorce or separation of stable unions. The proposal states that, in case of disagreement, a judge will determine custody based on factors such as cohabitation time, living conditions, care, and availability of time, and may also establish pet alimony and equally split veterinary expenses. Shared ownership applies only if most of the animal's life occurred during the union, and custody may be denied in cases of domestic violence, potentially resulting in loss of possession and ownership without compensation.
